About the Hackathon

What is Innovate 4 Impact?

Innovate 4 Impact is a national-level social impact innovation challenge organized by the IEEE Women in Engineering (WIE) Affinity Group, Bharati Vidyapeeth College of Engineering, Pune.

The event encourages students to develop innovative, scalable, and technology-driven solutions addressing real-world societal challenges — with a focus on healthcare, women's empowerment, and smart technology.

Open to all UG students across India. At least one female team member is mandatory — because real innovation is inclusive.

College Leadership
PrincipalDr. Rajesh Prasad
Vice PrincipalDr. Sachin Chavan
Vice PrincipalDr. Sunita Jadhav
Key Highlights
  • 🏆
    National Level Hackathon — UG students across India
  • 🆓
    100% FREE Registration — no hidden charges
  • 💰
    Prize Pool ₹15,000 + Goodies + Trophies
  • 🌐
    Hybrid Mode — Online rounds + Offline Grand Finale
  • 👥
    Team Size: 2–4 Members (min. 1 female mandatory)
  • 🏫
    All members from the same institution only
  • 🤖
    AI tools allowed with ownership responsibility
Problem Tracks

Hackathon Themes

Choose a track and define your own problem statement aligned with our themes. Open scope: Software · Hardware · AI/ML · IoT · Blockchain · Web & Mobile · Social Innovation

🏥

Healthcare, Wellbeing & Accessibility

Develop solutions that improve access to healthcare, mental wellbeing tools, assistive technologies, and innovations for differently-abled communities.

🛡️

Women's Safety & Empowerment

Build technology that enhances safety, enables economic empowerment, or creates platforms supporting gender equity and women's rights.

⚙️

Smart Technology & Innovation

Leverage AI/ML, IoT, Blockchain, or emerging tech to create intelligent systems addressing industrial or societal challenges.

Competition Format

3-Round Hybrid Challenge

A structured multi-round evaluation designed to test your innovation, technical skill, and real-world impact potential.

01
Online

Idea Screening

Submit your abstract and a PPT (7–9 slides) covering: Problem Statement, Proposed Solution, Innovation, Technical Approach, Social Impact, and Feasibility.

Abstract SubmissionPPT 7–9 SlidesPPT / PDF / Word
02
Online

Online Evaluation

Live online presentation or recorded video submission (5–7 minutes). Show your prototype progress, technical implementation, and expected impact.

Video Submission5–7 MinutesEnglish or HindiDrive / YouTube Link
03
Offline

Grand Finale

One-day offline finale at BV(DU)COE, Pune. Present a functional prototype, MVP, or demonstrable PoC — 8–10 min demo + 5 min judge Q&A.

Offline @ BV(DU)COE PuneFunctional Prototype / MVP8–10 Min DemoJudge Q&A

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Open to all undergraduate students currently enrolled in recognized institutions across India, from any engineering or interdisciplinary domain.
Yes! Registration is 100% FREE. There are absolutely no hidden charges or participation fees at any stage.
Teams must have 2–4 members. At least one female member is mandatory. All members must be from the same institution. Team changes are not permitted after Round 1 without organizer approval.
Yes, AI tools are permitted. However, teams must demonstrate full understanding and ownership of their solution. Excessive dependency without technical understanding may negatively impact evaluation scores.
Yes! Teams may select organizer-provided problem statements or propose their own innovative idea aligned with one of the three defined themes.
All updates will be shared via official email i.e bv.innovate4impact@gmail.com or from arvind4z@ieee.org , and the Unstop platform. Please monitor these channels regularly.
Bring your laptops, chargers, hardware components (if applicable), extension boards, and any accessories needed for your demo. Basic infrastructure will be provided by organizers.

Registering via Google Form?

Team ID is mandatory for all submissions
